key_insight: An unsupervised domain adaptation model with gradual source domain expansion can transfer weld penetration prediction across fundamentally different welding processes without requiring new labels.
engineering_relevance: This reduces relabeling effort and improves intelligent monitoring for welding lines that need robust quality prediction across TIG and laser systems.
method: Train a domain-adaptation classifier on labeled source welding data and unlabeled target welding data.
Use gradual source domain expansion to progressively include more source samples during adaptation.
Learn domain-invariant features while preserving class separability, verified with UMAP visualization.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports average accuracies of 90.65%/90.72% in same-process tests and 80.48%/81.13% in cross-process transfer, outperforming supervised baselines by about 35.83% to 43.40%.
watch_item: Performance may depend on the specific TIG and laser datasets used, so broader validation is needed before deployment on other welding setups or sensing conditions.
paper_title: A cross-process welding penetration status prediction algorithm based on unsupervised domain adaptation in laser and TIG welding
